evals.query: Classify this for transport — vague substance, no UN number, no mode.
evals.expected_behavior: Runs the intake; refuses to cross-walk until the substance, mode and packaging are captured; resolves the UN entry from the DG list, never assumed.
expectations: Runs the structured intake before any cross-walk.
Refuses to proceed on the vague request — elicits substance/mode/packaging.
Does not invent a UN number / class (citation accuracy).

evals.query: We need to ship this by rail and by air too — give me the rail and air classification.
evals.expected_behavior: Flags rail (RID) and air (IATA/ICAO-TI) as out of scope for v1.0 rather than guessing; covers the in-scope road/sea modes.
expectations: Flags rail (RID) and air (IATA/ICAO-TI) as out of scope — does NOT guess a rail/air classification.
Covers the in-scope modes (ADR/DOT road, IMDG sea) with the class/placard/segregation.
Cross-walks from the GHS class; [GAP]-flags any unknown UN entry.

evals.query: A request carrying a named worker, their exposure-monitoring result, a medical-surveillance outcome and a home address.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST; the name, contact, government ID, address and health detail are pseudonymised to SEG/role labels and aggregated; any verbatim leak is a de_identification hard-fail.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST — identifiers are detected and listed up front.
Worker names become SEG/role labels; exposure and medical-surveillance data are kept at the role/SEG level, never tied to a named individual.
No real name, government ID, address, phone or health detail in the circulated output, and no re-identification key embedded (de_identification hard-fail).
